factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: US Treasury yields falling on Fed dovish repricing. FXStreet reports bond yields declining as inflation concerns ease and Kevin Warsh's tough talk paradoxically lowered yields; lower real yields are structurally bullish for gold.

factors.name: Fed Policy Pivot
factors.summary: Minneapolis Fed President Kashkari now projects only ONE rate hike in 2026 (down from prior expectations), citing doubts over US-Iran peace deal and AI spending concerns. This dovish shift removes a major headwind for gold.

factors.name: US Dollar (DXY)
factors.summary: Greenback ended mixed on Friday and weakened alongside falling Treasury yields. FXStreet wrap notes USD momentum softened on Core PCE data; weaker dollar supports gold strength.

factors.name: Inflation Data (PCE)
factors.summary: Core PCE data came in softer than expected, triggering yield declines and equity weakness. Cooler inflation removes disinflation fears and supports dovish Fed narrative, gold-positive.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (US-Iran)
factors.summary: US conducted retaliatory strikes on Iran after drone attack on cargo ship Ever Lovely in Strait of Hormuz. Elevated tensions typically support safe-haven demand (bullish gold), but oil fell hard (-8.5% weekly), suggesting risk-off without traditional flight-to-safety intensity.

factors.name: Equities & Risk Sentiment
factors.summary: Nasdaq down 4.6% for the week; major tech sell-off driven by AI spending ROI doubts and Oracle's worst week since 2001 dot-com. Risk-off broadly, but equities weakness coincides with yield collapse—mixed signal for gold.

factors.name: Oil Prices (WTI)
factors.summary: Crude fell to $69.23, down 42.6% from $119.48 peak despite geopolitical tension. Oil weakness typically disinflationary; supports lower rate expectations but removes inflation-hedge component of gold narrative.

factors.name: Central Bank Gold Buying
factors.summary: No recent CB buying data in news window. Structural bid from PBoC, RBI, Turkey remains in place but not materially shifted by this week's events.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows
factors.summary: No GLD/IAU flow data in news window. Insufficient information to assess institutional positioning shift.

factors.name: Silver Price Action
factors.summary: Silver (XAG/USD) collapsed nearly 10% weekly, breaking below $60.00 for first time since December 2025 rally began. Weakness in silver typically signals risk-off liquidation, which can drag gold despite safe-haven bid.

factors.name: Upcoming NFP (July)
factors.summary: US jobs report is the major event ahead in the next 72h. Strong NFP could reinvigorate 'Fed on hold' narrative; weak data would confirm dovish turn and further support gold.

screening.narrative: Gold rallied +1.89% over 24h and +0.33% in 4h on the back of a dovish Fed repricing (Kashkari's one-hike projection, softer PCE, Warsh jawboning yields lower) and broad US Treasury yield declines. Risk-off sentiment from the tech sell-off and silver's -10% weekly collapse created initial safe-haven demand, but the move has already materialized—gold is in Phase 2 with +1.89% intraday gain. Geopolitical tensions (US-Iran strikes) add marginal support but are being offset by oil's 42.6% collapse, which removes inflation-hedge premium. The bias remains bullish on structural lower-real-yield story, but momentum is showing early signs of exhaustion.
catalysts_ahead: US NFP (Non-Farm Payroll) release likely early next week — will test whether dovish Fed pivot is validated or if labor market strength forces rate-hike repricing
ISM Manufacturing & Services PMI (next week) — key inflation/growth indicators; weak data = more dovish, strong data = re-hawkish risk
Eurozone inflation releases (next week) — ECB policy trajectory affects EUR/USD and broader DXY
Bank of England commentary & BoJ guidance — yen carry unwind risk if BoJ hikes further; USD/JPY near 162.00 is critical
FOMC minutes or Fed speaker schedule (if any this week) — any retreat from Kashkari's dovish tone would flip bias bearish
screening.risk_warning: If NFP comes in hot (>200k) or ISM Manufacturing surprises to upside, the 'one hike in 2026' narrative will be questioned and real yields could rebound sharply, cutting gold's legs out. Similarly, if US-Iran tensions escalate into broader conflict (offshore oil supply shock), oil re-rallies and inflation expectations spike—bullish for yields, bearish for gold. Equities finding a bid after this week's rout would also trigger a tactical pullback in gold's safe-haven premium. Finally, if silver stabilizes and reverses, it would confirm the current gold rally is not just liquidation-driven, validating the upside—lack of silver confirmation is a subtle warning.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already +1.89% in 24h; wait for pullback to $4,070–$4,080 support (Friday's 4h lows) before adding longs. If 4h close holds above $4,095, watch for test of $4,110–$4,120 resistance next week. For shorts, invalidation is a break above $4,130; do not chase downside until either NFP event risk passes or yields rebound materially (back above 4.2% on 10y nominal).

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: US Treasury yields falling sharply as investors trim hawkish Fed bets post-PCE (per FXStreet 19:54). Lower nominal yields with stable inflation expectations = lower real yields, structurally supportive for gold.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: Greenback weakening; FX strategists note USD momentum softened on PCE data (OCBC, FXStreet 21:58). Weaker dollar is directly bullish for gold priced in USD.

factors.name: Federal Reserve Policy Shift
factors.summary: Fed Chair Warsh talking tough on inflation but markets pricing in LOWER rate hikes this year (Kashkari now projects one hike vs prior expectations); dovish tilt emerging. Reduces near-term tightening pressure.

factors.name: Inflation Data (PCE)
factors.summary: PCE print came in cool enough to ease hawkish concerns; no hot surprise reported. Market relief visible in bond yields collapsing and equities rotation into healthcare (safer havens).

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (US-Iran Escalation)
factors.summary: US struck Iran in retaliation for drone/ship attack in Strait of Hormuz; fragile ceasefire tested (Al Jazeera multiple reports 00:00–04:13). Uncertainty/safe-haven demand typically supports gold. Oil initially up but settled lower ($69.23), limiting inflation spiral fears.

factors.name: Equity Market Weakness
factors.summary: NASDAQ down 4.60% for the week (5 consecutive down days); tech capitulation and AI spending doubts domino (MarketWatch 21:16). Risk-off regime favors gold as safe-haven; flight to quality ongoing.

factors.name: Oil Prices (Brent/WTI)
factors.summary: Oil settled at $69.23 Friday (third consecutive weekly loss); Strait of Hormuz attacks contained via ship traffic resumption (Kriskey commentary). Not triggering stagflation fears; mutes inflation upside risk for gold.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows & Central Bank Buying
factors.summary: No fresh flow data in news window. Structural CB bid ongoing but not highlighted; insufficient detail to score.

factors.name: Upcoming Catalysts (US Jobs Report)
factors.summary: US jobs report due this week (likely Friday); significant NFP miss or beat could shift Fed rate-cut odds and real yields. Event risk remains, capping near-term conviction.

screening.narrative: Gold is benefiting from a confluence of lower real yields, USD weakness, and equity risk-off after PCE cooled Fed hawkish bets and NASDAQ fell 4.6% for the week. US-Iran escalation adds safe-haven bid. However, the move is already underway (+1.89% in 24h, +0.08% in 4h), putting price action in early Phase 2; rally is nascent but not yet extended. Upcoming US jobs report and continued equity volatility are key near-term wildcards that could snap conviction either way.
catalysts_ahead: US Non-Farm Payroll (NFP) – likely Friday this week; critical for Fed rate-cut trajectory
US jobless claims (weekly) – ongoing, shapes labor-market narrative
Geopolitical escalation updates: Iran retaliation risk, Strait of Hormuz disruption severity, Israel-Lebanon framework enforcement
Fed speakers (Kashkari, Waller, Williams) – any fresh dovish/hawkish commentary shifts rate-cut odds
FOMC meeting minutes or next scheduled decision – could reset inflation/rate expectations
screening.risk_warning: If NFP surprise comes in HOT (significantly above expectations), Fed will slow rate-cut pace, real yields will rise, and gold will face headwinds. Alternatively, if equity bounce is sharp (VIX collapses), risk-on flows could reverse safe-haven demand. US-Iran ceasefire restoration would also reduce geopolitical premium. Watch DXY closely: if dollar reverses higher on hawkish surprise, gold downside is unprotected.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early Phase 2 (+1.89% already captured); enter on pullbacks to $4,085–$4,095 (support zone near recent lows) rather than chasing higher. NFP Friday is a major event risk—consider waiting for post-data stabilization before new long entries to avoid whipsaw.
